name: rule id namespacing: deprecated rule's replaced_by entries must reference rule ids that exist somewhere in the active rule set (no dangling references)
command:
mkdir -p tree/google && printf 'source:
  id: g
  authoritative_publisher: G
  authoritative_url: https://g.com
  registry_link: STANDARDS-URLS.yaml
  fetched_at: 2026-05-04T00:00:00Z
  content_hash: sha256:S
  fetch_frequency: daily
  fragility_tier: low
  license_note: MIT
rules:
  - {id: g-old-001, name: x, description: x, detector: x, type: problem, fixable: null, has_suggestions: false, deprecated: true, replaced_by: [g-vanished-999], docs_url: https://x.com, requires_type_checking: false, recommended: false, options_schema: {}, messages: {default: x}, severity: P1, version: 1.0.0, semver: 1.0.0, rule_state: disabled, provenance: [{source: x, class: published-standard, section_id: x, url: https://x.com, fetched_at: 2026-05-04T00:00:00Z, content_hash: sha256:S}]}
recommended_set: []
all_set: [g-old-001]
' > tree/google/x.yaml && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/aggregator.sh" --root "$PWD/tree" --check-replaced-by-references
